---
title: "SA Housing Market: Interactive Price Trends"
description: "Exploring residential property price trends across South African metros using R and interactive Plotly charts."
date: 2025-02-20
categories: [R, Plotly, Interactive, Real Estate, South Africa]
image: thumbnail.png
---

## Overview

This post explores simulated residential property price indices across four
major South African metros. The charts here are **fully interactive** — hover
to see values, click the legend to toggle metros, and zoom in on any period.

---

## Setup

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| message: false
#| warning: false

library(tidyverse)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
```

---

## Data

```{r}
#| label: data

set.seed(7)

metros <- c("Johannesburg", "Cape Town", "Durban", "Pretoria")
quarters <- seq(as.Date("2015-01-01"), as.Date("2024-10-01"), by = "quarter")

# Simulate price index (base = 100 at start) with different growth paths
df <- expand_grid(metro = metros, date = quarters) |>
  arrange(metro, date) |>
  group_by(metro) |>
  mutate(
    base_growth = case_when(
      metro == "Cape Town"     ~ 0.025,
      metro == "Johannesburg"  ~ 0.015,
      metro == "Pretoria"      ~ 0.018,
      metro == "Durban"        ~ 0.010
    ),
    noise       = cumsum(rnorm(n(), 0, 0.8)),
    index       = 100 * cumprod(1 + base_growth / 4 + rnorm(n(), 0, 0.005))
  ) |>
  ungroup()
```

---

## Interactive Price Index Chart

```{r}
#| label: fig-interactive
#| fig-cap: "Residential property price index by metro (hover to explore)"

plot_ly(
  df,
  x     = ~date,
  y     = ~round(index, 1),
  color = ~metro,
  type  = "scatter",
  mode  = "lines",
  hovertemplate = "<b>%{fullData.name}</b><br>%{x|%b %Y}<br>Index: %{y}<extra></extra>"
) |>
  layout(
    title   = list(text = "SA Residential Property Price Index (Base = 100, Q1 2015)"),
    xaxis   = list(title = ""),
    yaxis   = list(title = "Price Index"),
    legend  = list(title = list(text = "Metro")),
    hovermode = "x unified"
  )
```

---

## Interactive Bar: Latest Index Values

```{r}
#| label: fig-bar
#| fig-cap: "Latest index value per metro"

latest <- df |>
  group_by(metro) |>
  slice_max(date, n = 1)

plot_ly(
  latest,
  x    = ~reorder(metro, index),
  y    = ~round(index, 1),
  type = "bar",
  color = ~metro,
  text = ~paste0(round(index, 1)),
  textposition = "outside",
  hovertemplate = "<b>%{x}</b><br>Index: %{y}<extra></extra>"
) |>
  layout(
    title  = "Latest Residential Price Index by Metro",
    xaxis  = list(title = ""),
    yaxis  = list(title = "Index Value"),
    showlegend = FALSE
  )
```

---

## Takeaways

- **Cape Town** has seen the strongest cumulative price growth since 2015
- **Durban** has lagged significantly, with the lowest current index
- All metros show post-2020 acceleration followed by softening into 2024

::: {.callout-tip}
## Interact with the charts
Click a city name in the legend to hide/show it. Drag on the chart to zoom in.
Double-click to reset the view.
:::
